0.content:
## MONOLOGUE / INNER THOUGHT — [THINK|Name|#hexcolor|the thought]
Surface a character's private inner thought as a bubble on its OWN line.
- {Name}: who is thinking — MUST exactly match the name in that character's [CHAR] header (include life-stage form).
- {#hexcolor}: MUST match that character's [CHAR]/[SAY] color; default #cda64e if unknown.
- {the thought}: first-person, in {{user}}'s language. Do NOT use the | or ] characters inside the thought (they break the bubble) — rephrase.

## ABSOLUTE PLACEMENT RULE — MONOLOGUE ONLY DIRECTLY ON TOP OF ITS HEADER
[THINK] is printed on the line IMMEDIATELY ABOVE that SAME character's [CHAR] header. They TOUCH.
CORRECT:
[THINK|Name|#hex|...inner thought...]
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex]
[SAY|#hex|...spoken words...]

DO NOT DO — monologue, then narrative, then header:
[THINK|Name|#hex|...]
He turned toward the voice.        <- FORBIDDEN: no narration between THINK and CHAR
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex]

DO NOT DO — header, narrative, then monologue:
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex]
He turned toward the voice.        <- FORBIDDEN
[THINK|Name|#hex|...]              <- FORBIDDEN: monologue must be ABOVE the header, never below

RULES:
- Use it whenever a present character is genuinely reflecting and their inner state matters; each thinking character gets its own [THINK] directly above its own [CHAR].
- {{user}} writes their OWN inner voice as plain italics; NEVER put thoughts in {{user}}'s head.
- Keep it short, in-character, first-person. Brackets render as a bubble — never print them as literal text.

## SELF-CHECK (every [THINK] must pass)
1. Is it on the line IMMEDIATELY above its [CHAR] header, with NOTHING between them?
2. Do Name and #hex EXACTLY match that header (and the [SAY] color)?
3. Is the thought first-person, in {{user}}'s language, free of | and ] ?
If any fails, fix order/name/color BEFORE sending. A [THINK] below the header or split from it by narration is WRONG.

1.content:
## CHARACTER HEADER — [CHAR|image|Name|#hexcolor]
Print this avatar+name header immediately before a character speaks or acts.
- {image}: catbox.moe filename (e.g. abc123.png) — system prepends https://files.catbox.moe/. No image -> leave xxxxxx.png (the framed border stays, image hides).
- {Name}: canonical name (keep consistent across languages; include any life-stage form, e.g. "Name (Adult)").
- {#hexcolor}: that character's signature color — MUST match the color used in their [THINK] and [SAY].

## THE SPEECH BLOCK (HEADER IS THE MIDDLE LINE) — STRICT, USED TOGETHER
The three tags form ONE inseparable block printed as CONSECUTIVE lines in this EXACT order:
[THINK|Name|#hex|inner thought]   <- Monologue  (ONLY when the character is thinking; sits directly ABOVE this header)
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex]            <- this Header
[SAY|#hex|spoken words]           <- Dialogue   (sits directly BELOW this header)
- NOTHING may be inserted between the lines — no narration, action, or blank prose between [THINK]&[CHAR] or [CHAR]&[SAY].
- Narration/action goes BEFORE the block (scene-setting) or AFTER the [SAY] bubble — never between the tags.
- One header per speaker turn; for back-and-forth, repeat the whole block per speaker.

2.content:
## DIALOGUE / SPEECH BUBBLE — [SAY|#hexcolor|the spoken words]
ALL character speech MUST use this bubble. NEVER write spoken lines as plain quoted "text".
- {#hexcolor}: the speaker's signature color — MUST match their [CHAR] header (and [THINK]).
- {the spoken words}: exactly what is said, in {{user}}'s roleplay language. Avoid | and ] inside (they break the bubble).

## PLACEMENT — DIALOGUE IS THE LAST LINE OF THE SPEECH BLOCK
[SAY] sits DIRECTLY BELOW the speaker's [CHAR] header, touching it:
[THINK|Name|#hex|inner thought]   (Monologue — only if thinking; directly above the header)
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex]            (Header)
[SAY|#hex|spoken words]           (this Dialogue bubble)
- NEVER insert narration/action/blank prose between [CHAR] and [SAY] (or between [THINK] and [CHAR]).
- Narration, description and action come AFTER the [SAY] bubble (or before the whole block) — never between the tags.
RULES:
- One bubble per spoken turn; multiple sentences may share one bubble. Repeat the full block per speaker for back-and-forth.
- Only render NPC/character speech; {{user}}'s lines are typed by the player. Inner thoughts use [THINK], not a bubble.
- Names and colors stay canonical regardless of language; only spoken words translate.
